feed.title: SAP Community - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Extensibility
feed.subtitle: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Extensibility Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Unable to Map Place of Supply Field in Journal Entry Post SOAP API (SAP_COM_0002)
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

The "Place of Supply" field is not available in the Journal Entry – Post (Synchronous) SOAP API payload. We need to automate the posting process, and Create Outgoing Invoices is not the optimal solution. This gap is causing data inconsistency and impacting GST compliance in India. It is confirmed with SAP Support and this Influence Portal that the "Place of Supply" field is currently not available in API Journal Entry Post.

Customer Influence: Place of supply missing in Journal Entry - Post (Asynchronous)

Steps tried:

  • Reviewed API payload and I_JOURNALENTRYTP interface - field not found
  • Checked BAdIs - no relevant enhancement spot
  • Reviewed SAP API Hub docs - no reference to this field

Please let me know the workaround to include "Place of Supply" via API. Thank you so much.

entry.title: Not able to get CDS for tax rates based on tax jurisdiction
entry.content:
Dear all,

 

Further to SAP Note 3636847 – “Tax item information on Purchase Order Output Forms - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public”,

we are currently implementing Workaround 1 (i.e., implementing the BAdI to calculate values using custom logic).

However, for tax jurisdiction–based countries, we are unable to find a CDS view for the relevant tax codes rates based on the tax jurisdiction. The CDS view I_TAXCODERATE does not contain tax rates for such tax jurisdiction–based scenarios.

Could you please advise which CDS view should be used to retrieve tax code rates for tax jurisdiction–based countries?

Thanks in advance

Suddy

entry.title: How to edit Inspection Lot Short Text after Usage Decision (UD)
entry.content:
Hi Team,


We need a solution to allow users to update or enter the inspection lot short text after Usage Decision (UD) has been completed.

Currently, if a user enters incorrect text or misses this field, the field becomes read-only after the UD is made. This short text is mandatory for internal reporting and printing layouts.

Is there a standard SAP Fiori app or any other option that allows unlocking this short text field post-UD?

If no standard option exists, what is the SAP-recommended Cloud Badi, Developer Extensibility approach, API to safely update this short text on the inspection lot?

entry.title: Additional field in Manage G/L Account Master Data
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support,

We are working on harmonizing our chart of accounts in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and would like to maintain, at the G/L Account master data level, an additional field referencing the legacy account number used by our company prior to harmonization. We would therefore like to know whether it is possible to add a custom field to the application "Manage G/L Account Master Data" for this purpose, visible across all company codes (Chart of Accounts level).

Could you please confirm:

Whether such a customization (via the Custom Fields app or another means) is technically feasible for this application, and if so, which Business Context should be used.
Whether this service is chargeable.
Whether there is an existing standard field  that would meet this requirement, or any alternative standard solutions/future roadmap enhancements addressing it.

 

Thank you

entry.title: BAdI: Collection Basic Rule not getting customer to collection worklist
entry.content:
Hi SAP,

There is a user requirement to get all customers into Process collection worklist app regardless the customer has overdue or not yet due items. We implemented BAdI: Collection Basic Rule to test getting 1 customer into Process collection worklist but it does not work. Can you advise it the BADI support this requirement. Thank you.

Regards,

Leslie

entry.title: Output Parameter Determination: extend the condition parameters to include standard fields
entry.content:
Hi Anyone,

This specific example relates to the “Handling Unit” business rule. The goal is to be able to print material-specific HU labels. The material/product ID should be used for this purpose. Product: SAP Public Cloud!

The standard field “Material” is not available in the table column selection:
The result list contains 39 parameters but material is not available. Is there a way to extend the condition parameters to include standard fields?

 

What we’ve already tried—extending with a custom field:
We created a custom field named “YY1_MATERIAL” and copied the standard field using a BADI. Unfortunately, the only BADI is executed too late and only allows changes at the time the form is output.

Best regards

entry.title: Add Custom Fields in Look Up Retail Products app
entry.content:
I want to add custom fields under Information tab of Look Up Retail Products app.

 

I create custom fields in Custom Fields app with Business Context: Look up product information in retail stores (RFM_STORE_LKPPROD).

But from AdaptUI, custom field is not appear. Why?

Best Regards,

Khaing Zin

entry.title: Consumption of CBO created in Customizing tenant by ABAP / Dev extensibility created CDS view?
entry.content:
Can a Custom Business Object created in the Customizing tenant (key user extensibility) be consumed by an ABAP CDS view created in the Development tenant / Eclipse (developer extensibility)?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: How to hide "Post" button for general users in Create Supplier Invoice / Supplier Invoices List / MI
entry.content:
**Title:** How to hide "Post" button for general users in Create Supplier Invoice / Supplier Invoices List / MIRO?

**Body:**

We have a customer requirement to hide the "Post" button for general (non-approver) users in the following apps:
- Create Supplier Invoice (F0859)
- Supplier Invoices List (F1060A)
- MIRO

**Business background:**
The intended process is:
1. General user creates a supplier invoice and clicks "Save as Completed"
2. Approver reviews and approves via My Inbox

General users should never be able to post directly. We already have a BAdI implementation (MRM_CHECK_INVOICE_CLOUD) that blocks direct posting with an error message when the invoice status isn't "Parked and completed." However, the customer specifically wants the Post button to be **hidden**, not just blocked with an error.

**What we've tried so far:**

1. **Removing Business Catalogs** SAP_FIN_BC_AA_POST_REG_PC and SAP_FIN_BC_AA_DOC_PROC_PC from the role. This does hide the Post button, but other unrelated apps tied to the same catalogs also lose access as a side effect. We'd like to avoid this catalog-level approach due to that collateral impact.

2. **Using IAM App Authorization Variants** (available since 2502) to control access at a more granular level than the catalog. We found two variants tied to transaction F0859:
- F0859_TRAN ("Create Supplier Invoice") — description states this variant allows "processing and posting selection"
- F0859_77_TRAN ("Hold Supplier Invoice") — description states this variant allows "processing and holding (not posting)"

We deactivated F0859_TRAN while keeping F0859_77_TRAN active, expecting the Post button to disappear while Park/Save as Completed remained available. However, **the Post button is still displayed** after deactivation. Is this the wrong IAM App to target? Is there a different/additional app or restriction type that actually controls Post button visibility?

**Additional question on MIRO:**
Our environment also has the classic MIRO transaction. It doesn't appear when we search the IAM App list for "supplier invoice" apps tied to F0859. Does MIRO's Post authorization come from a completely separate business catalog/IAM app? If so, which one, and does it need to be addressed separately from the Fiori Create Supplier Invoice app?

**Environment:** SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Release 2508 (or later)

Any guidance on the correct approach (catalog restriction, IAM App Authorization Variant, or something else) — and confirmation on how MIRO fits into this — would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Can alternative reconciliation account and posting control (workflow / BAdI) be combined for supplie
entry.content:
Can alternative reconciliation account and posting control (workflow / BAdI) be combined for supplier invoices in Public Cloud?

**Body:**

I'd like to confirm my understanding of the following matrix for supplier invoice entry in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. Could someone with product knowledge confirm whether this is correct, or point out any mistakes?

| App | PO 3-way match | Alternative recon. account | Approval Workflow | Posting control (BAdI/standard) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Create Supplier Invoice (F0859)** — MM | Yes | No | Yes | Yes (BAdI MRM_CHECK_INVOICE_CLOUD) |
| **Create Supplier Invoice - Advanced / MIRO** — MM | Yes | No | Yes | Yes (BAdI MRM_CHECK_INVOICE_CLOUD) |
| **Create Incoming Invoices (FB60)** — FI | No | Yes | No (not available in Public) | No |

My understanding behind each cell:
- Alternative reconciliation account can only be set in the FI app (FB60), because it is an FI function. In MM invoices (F0859 / MIRO), the reconciliation account field is greyed out.
- BAdI MRM_CHECK_INVOICE_CLOUD only applies to MM Invoice Verification (LIV), so it does not fire for FB60.
- The approval workflow (Flexible Workflow for Supplier Invoices) works with F0859 / MIRO but is not integrated with FB60 in Public Edition.

**My main question:**
Based on the above, it appears that in Public Edition there is **no standard way to use an alternative reconciliation account while also enforcing posting control via workflow or BAdI** — because the alternative reconciliation account requires FB60 (FI), but posting control (workflow / MRM BAdI) only works with MM invoices (F0859 / MIRO). Is this correct? Is there any standard method, configuration, or supported approach to achieve both at the same time?

**Environment:** SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Release 2508 (or later)

Any confirmation or correction based on documentation or project experience would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: **Title:** Alternative reconciliation account works in Create Supplier Invoice (F0859) but fails in
entry.content:
**Title:** Alternative reconciliation account works in Create Supplier Invoice (F0859) but fails in MIRO (Msg F5439)

**Body:**

We are configuring the alternative reconciliation account function in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. It works in the Create Supplier Invoice (F0859) app, but fails in **MIRO (Create Supplier Invoice - Advanced)** in all cases. I'd appreciate help understanding why.

**Configuration we have done:**

1. **SSCUI 102631 (Define Alternative Reconciliation Accounts):** defined the alternative reconciliation accounts allowed for the standard reconciliation account.
2. **SSCUI 103508 (Define Account Group):** since the standard account group ABST cannot be modified, we created a new account group **ZZST** by copying ABST, and set the "reconciliation account ready for input" indicator as optional.
3. **G/L Account Master (Fiori ID F0731A):** assigned account group **ZZST** to both the reconciliation account G/L and the alternative reconciliation account G/L.

**Test results:**

| App | Invoice type | Result |
|---|---|---|
| Create Supplier Invoice (F0859) | G/L account item-based | OK |
| Create Supplier Invoice (F0859) | PO reference-based | OK |
| MIRO (Create Supplier Invoice - Advanced) | G/L account item-based | **Error (Msg F5439)** |
| MIRO (Create Supplier Invoice - Advanced) | PO reference-based | **Error (Msg F5439)** |

**Error message (F5439):**
"Mark the G/L reconciliation account as ready for input" — the message states that to enter a reconciliation account when posting a document, the account must be marked as 'reconciliation account ready for input' in the master, and that this condition is not met. The entered account is therefore not allowed.

**My question:**
With the exact same configuration, the alternative reconciliation account works correctly in F0859 (both G/L-based and PO-based invoices), but MIRO raises F5439 in every case. Why does MIRO behave differently from F0859 despite identical configuration? Is there an additional setting required specifically for MIRO, or does MIRO not support the alternative reconciliation account in Public Edition?

**Environment:** SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Release 2602

Any guidance based on documentation or project experience would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Change field selection in "Create Customer Project"
entry.content:
Dear Community user,

Background: In our project, there are hundreds of cost center in the system.
So, when our customer enter the Customer project or Internal project in the system, our customer wants to search Costcenter in the selection field.

Current Situation: The SAP Standard "Create Customer Project" does not provide search selection same as Project Manager or Customer field in the left side.
Also, When I put the costcenter code directly in the cost center field, the suggestion is not working.
Our customer is using Japanese language, english description search is working but Japanese description search is not working in current SAP verstion

Kindly share knowledge how to change this cost center field?

Best Regards,

entry.title: Internal Manage Early Numbering
entry.content:
METHOD earlynumbering_create.
    "Declare Variable First
    DATA: number_range_key               TYPE cl_numberrange_runtime=>nr_number,
          number_range_return_code       TYPE cl_numberrange_runtime=>nr_returncode,
          number_range_returned_quantity TYPE cl_numberrange_runtime=>nr_quantity.

    LOOP AT entities into DATA(ls_entity).
        "Try Calling The Method First
        TRY.
            cl_numberrange_runtime=>number_get(
              EXPORTING
                nr_range_nr       = '01'
                object            = 'ZCLA'
                quantity          = 1
                toyear            = ls_entity-Fiscalyear
              IMPORTING
                number            = number_range_key
                returncode        = number_range_return_code
                returned_quantity = number_range_returned_quantity
            ).
          "Catch the error into the message
          CATCH cx_nr_object_not_found INTO DATA(lx_not_found).
              APPEND VALUE #(  %cid      = ls_entity-%cid
                               %key      = ls_entity-%key
                               %msg      = lx_not_found
                            ) TO reported-zi_cla_exp_header.
              APPEND VALUE #(  %cid      = ls_entity-%cid
                               %key      = ls_entity-%key
                            ) TO failed-zi_cla_exp_header.
            RETURN.
          CATCH cx_number_ranges INTO DATA(lx_ranges).
            APPEND VALUE #(  %cid      = ls_entity-%cid
                               %key      = ls_entity-%key
                               %msg      = lx_ranges
                            ) TO reported-zi_cla_exp_header.
              APPEND VALUE #(  %cid      = ls_entity-%cid
                               %key      = ls_entity-%key
                            ) TO failed-zi_cla_exp_header.
            RETURN.
        ENDTRY.
        ls_entity-NumClaim = number_range_key+14(6).
        "Append value to the table
        APPEND VALUE #( %cid      = ls_entity-%cid
                        %key      = ls_entity-%key
                      ) TO mapped-zi_cla_exp_header.
    ENDLOOP.

  ENDMETHOD.

 

I have created  an early numbering implementation method like this. But it seems when I want to create data, it always output an error like below

I've already tried manual numbering insert by hard code it in the method. What should i do? Is there anyway i can check the append to the mapped-zi_cla_exp_header.?

I will attached the table definition also 

@EndUserText.label : 'Custom Table for Claim Expense Header'
@AbapCatalog.enhancement.category : #NOT_EXTENSIBLE
@AbapCatalog.tableCategory : #TRANSPARENT
@AbapCatalog.deliveryClass : #A
@AbapCatalog.dataMaintenance : #RESTRICTED
define table zcla_exp_h {

  key client         : abap.clnt not null;
  key num_claim      : abap.numc(6) not null;
  name               : abap.char(64);
  description        : abap.char(128);
  datefrom           : abap.dats;
  dateto             : abap.dats;
  fiscalyear         : gjahr;
  companycode        : bukrs;
  costcenter         : kostl;
  created_by         : abp_creation_user;
  created_at         : abp_creation_tstmpl;
  last_changed_by    : abp_lastchange_user;
  last_changed_at    : abp_lastchange_tstmpl;
  local_last_changed : abp_locinst_lastchange_tstmpl;

}
@EndUserText.label : 'Custom Table for Claim Expense Item'
@AbapCatalog.enhancement.category : #NOT_EXTENSIBLE
@AbapCatalog.tableCategory : #TRANSPARENT
@AbapCatalog.deliveryClass : #A
@AbapCatalog.dataMaintenance : #RESTRICTED
define table zcla_exp_item {

  key client         : abap.clnt not null;
  key num_claim      : abap.numc(6) not null;
  key fiscalyear     : gjahr not null;
  key item_num       : abap.numc(5) not null;
  date_post          : abap.dats;
  event_description  : abap.char(128);
  category           : abap.char(64);
  currency           : waers;
  amount             : abap.dec(23,2);
  local_last_changed : abp_locinst_lastchange_tstmpl;

}

Here is the behavior definition also 

managed implementation in class zbp_i_cla_exp_header unique;
strict ( 2 );

define behavior for ZI_CLA_EXP_HEADER //alias <alias_name>
persistent table zcla_exp_h
lock master
authorization master ( instance )
early numbering
//etag master <field_name>
{
  create ( authorization : global );
  update;
  delete;
  field ( readonly ) NumClaim;
  association _items { create; }
}

define behavior for ZI_CLA_EXP_ITEM //alias <alias_name>
persistent table zcla_exp_item
lock dependent by _header
authorization dependent by _header
//etag master <field_name>
{
  update;
  delete;
  field ( readonly ) NumClaim;
  association _header;
}

 

Thank you for the attention

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

entry.title: Confirmation Required: Released API/CDS View for Customer Hierarchy Change Documents
entry.content:
Hello SAP Support Team,We are currently designing an outbound integration from SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud to an external system to send Customer Hierarchy data.As there is no standard API available for Customer Hierarchies, our current approach is to create a custom CDS view based on the standard CDS views:

  • I_CustUnivHierarchy
  • I_CustUnivHierarchyNode


This custom CDS view would then be exposed as a custom API to the external system.However, we have a delta requirement for this interface. The external system should receive only:

  • Newly created hierarchy records
  • Updated hierarchy records
  • Deleted hierarchy records


The challenge is that the CDS views I_CustUnivHierarchy and I_CustUnivHierarchyNode do not appear to provide change tracking information, such as creation date, last changed date, deletion indicator, or change type.In the Manage Customer Hierarchies Fiori app, we can see change history information

entry.title: Unable to configure outbound connectivity for API_PRODUCT_SRV in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

We are currently working on a requirement in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition related to Production Order documentation.

Business Requirement

The business needs to populate the inspection text maintained in the material master into the "Documents Libératoires" section of the Production Order form.

Since the inspection text is maintained on the product and needs to be available during production execution, we investigated several standard and extensibility options.

Analysis Performed

1. Text Determination

We initially explored standard Text Determination functionality to copy text from Material/BOM to the Production Order.

After discussions with the functional team, this approach was not suitable for retrieving the required inspection text.

2. CDS-Based Solution

We then analyzed available CDS views to retrieve the inspection text.

However, we could not identify a CDS view exposing this information, and SAP confirmed that no suitable CDS view is currently available.

3. Custom Field Workaround

As an alternative, we created a custom field to store the required inspection information so that it would be available through CDS-based reporting and extensions.

Unfortunately, the value is not consistently updated after saving the product, making this approach unreliable.

4. SAP Recommended Approach

SAP recommended retrieving the data through API_PRODUCT_SRV.

We therefore started implementing an outbound integration scenario.

Technical Steps Performed

Development

  1. Created package Z_DEV_API_SUB_PKG
  2. Defined outbound service ZINS_OUT_SRV_REST
  3. Created communication scenario ZINSP_CS
  4. Downloaded the EDMX metadata from SAP Business Accelerator Hub
  5. Generated Service Consumption Model ZINSP_TEXT_SCM_1
  6. Generated proxy class ZCLINSP_TEXT_SCM_1
  7. Developed ABAP console application ZCL_INSPECTION_TEXT

Configuration

  1. Created communication user CPI_PP_PROD
  2. Created communication system CPI_PRODUCT_INTEGRATION
  3. Started configuring the communication arrangement

Current Issue

During the outbound connectivity configuration, we are unable to identify how to create or assign the required outbound user for the API call.

This is preventing us from completing the integration based on API_PRODUCT_SRV.

Questions

  1. Has anyone successfully consumed API_PRODUCT_SRV from custom ABAP in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?
  2. What is the correct approach for configuring outbound connectivity and authentication in this scenario?
  3. Is there a standard communication scenario that should be used instead of a custom one?
  4. How should the outbound user be created or assigned when consuming API_PRODUCT_SRV from ABAP Cloud?
  5. Is there an alternative recommended approach to retrieve inspection text maintained in the product master?

Any guidance, configuration examples, or lessons learned would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you for your support.

Best regards.

entry.title: How to Link Two Analytical Queries in Embedded SAC App? "A linked dimension" Error
entry.content:
How to Link Two Analytical Queries in Embedded SAC App?
"A linked dimension for this model is not included in the visualization's dimension bindings" Error

I am creating an Embedded SAC Application in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and trying to link two Analytical Queries using Company Code. Both Company Code fields have the same data type, semantics, and values, and the link is created successfully.

However, the widget shows the error:

"A linked dimension for this model is not included in the visualization's dimension bindings."

How can I correctly connect/link these two queries? Is there any additional configuration required for linked dimensions or dimension bindings?

entry.title: SAP Public Cloud 2608 / Transfer of Custom Field into Billing Documents not working
entry.content:
Hello Experts,
my goal is to transfer the value of a custom field starting from the sales order to the billing document. So far the value is succesfully transfered from the sales order to the Billing Due List Items (as its the same object):



But when i create the Billing Document the transfer of the field value doesnt work:



For troubleshooting, i activated all Business Scenarios and User Interfaces for the following Business Contexts, but it doesnt help.



From my research the right business scenario controls the "copy control" for this custom field, but since i activated all i am out of ideas why this doesnt work. 
Im thankfull for any suggestions or other ways to troubleshoot this issue.

Best regards 
Lukas

entry.title: AddressLine1-8 formatting in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Form Templates
entry.content:
In our S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition implementation, we are currently performing customization development for form templates.
I would like to consult with you regarding the output of address information on these forms.

I understand that the address values for each form template are contained within the fields AddressLine1 through AddressLine8 in the XML.

Additionally, after checking the SAP Help site,

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/f86dc2eb1f8b48c880a7607213104b27/b86c68c2371c472796b6b54c9502ae97.html

I recognize that the output values and positions for AddressLine1 to AddressLine8 are determined by the following conditions, and that international/domestic addresses are output based on the address notation:

1. The length of the address is compressed based on priorities defined for each address and address type element.
2. Addresses are formatted based on current domestic and international guidelines:
・International address patterns of the Universal Postal Union (UPU)
・Regulations of each country
In this case, it is impossible to predict which AddressLine will contain the company name or specific address items.
For instance, if there is a customer requirement to make only the company name bold or increase its font size, or to separate the output positions of the company name and the address, we must identify the specific AddressLine for the company name.

Do you know any method to identify what value is stored in which AddressLine?
Also, if there is a way to achieve the above requirements using the AddressLine items, I would like to know.

Thank you.

entry.title: Consumption Tax (Input/Output VAT) in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: Complete Posting Flow from Invo
entry.content:
Consumption Tax (Input/Output VAT) in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: Complete Posting Flow from Invoice to Clearing — Which SSCUI Configures What?

**Product:** SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (2508 / 2602 / 2608)
**Area:** Finance — General Ledger / AR / AP / Tax
**Country/Region:** Japan (JP), Chart of Accounts **YCOA**

---

## Background

I'm an FI consultant currently running Fit-to-Standard workshops for a Japanese customer on S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. The customer asked what I thought was a simple question:

> "Walk us through every journal entry involving consumption tax, from invoice creation through to clearing. And tell us exactly where each one is configured."

I couldn't find a single consolidated source for this, so I pieced it together myself. Below is what I've established. I'd appreciate corrections and answers to the open questions at the end.

A recurring misconception I want to address up front, because I initially fell for a variant of it myself:

> **Consumption tax does NOT move at payment clearing.**

There is no "behind the scenes" tax transfer at clearing the way there is with, say, prepaid expense amortisation. Japan taxes on an accrual basis, so SAP finalises the tax amount at invoice posting and the balance simply sits on the balance sheet until it is manually transferred at declaration time.

---

## 1. Summary Table

| # | Event | Tax posted? | Accounts involved |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Down payment **request** (Sp. G/L ind. **F**) | **No** — noted item, no G/L posting at all | — |
| 2 | Down payment (Sp. G/L ind. **A**) | **No in Japan** (config-dependent) | MVA/VVA mechanism exists for other countries |
| 3 | **Customer invoice posting** | **Yes** | Output tax (**MWS**) |
| 4 | **Supplier invoice posting** | **Yes** | Input tax (**VST**) |
| 5 | Incoming / outgoing payment clearing | **No** | — |
| 6 | Clearing with cash discount | Yes (tax adjustment) | Cash discount + tax adjustment |
| 7 | Foreign currency clearing | **No** (tax fixed in document currency) | Exchange rate difference (KDF) only |
| 8 | **Tax declaration transfer** | **Yes (manual)** | Output/Input tax → Tax payable |

---

## 2. Normal AR / AP Cycle

### 2.1 Output Tax — Transaction Key **MWS**

**Step 1: Invoice posting** (`Create Billing Documents` / `Create Outgoing Invoices`)

```
Dr Accounts Receivable 1,100
Cr Revenue 1,000
Cr Output Tax (MWS) 100
```

Determination path for SD-driven postings:

```
Condition type (MWST etc.)
→ Account key MWS
→ G/L account (Output Tax)
```

For direct FI postings, the tax code entered on the line item resolves MWS → G/L account directly.

**Step 2: Incoming payment clearing** (`Post Incoming Payments`)

```
Dr Bank 1,100
Cr Accounts Receivable 1,100
```

That's it. **Output tax does not appear.**

### 2.2 Input Tax — Transaction Key **VST**

**Step 1: Supplier invoice posting** (`Create Supplier Invoice` / `Create Incoming Invoices`)

```
Dr Purchases / Expense 1,000
Dr Input Tax (VST) 100
Cr Accounts Payable 1,100
```

**Step 2: Outgoing payment clearing** (`Post Outgoing Payments` / `Manage Automatic Payments`)

```
Dr Accounts Payable 1,100
Cr Bank 1,100
```

Again — **no tax movement.**

---

## 3. The Exceptions (where tax *does* move outside invoice posting)

### 3.1 Down Payment Tax Clearing — **MVA / VVA**

This is the mechanism people are usually thinking of when they intuit "there must be an offsetting tax account somewhere."

**First, an important distinction:**

A **down payment request** (Sp. G/L indicator **F**) is a **noted item**. Per SAP Help: it functions as a reference document for the down payment posting or as a document for the dunning program, and **account balances are not updated**. Neither transaction figures nor account balances are managed on these accounts — you can only view them via line item display.

So a DP request posts **nothing at all** — not tax, not receivables. This is standard behaviour worldwide, not a Japanese specialty. A tax code *is* entered and carried forward to the subsequent down payment posting, but nothing hits the G/L.

**The actual down payment** (Sp. G/L indicator **A**) is where the tax clearing account appears — in countries where a down payment triggers a VAT liability (Germany, Russia, Ukraine etc.):

```
[Step 1] Down payment received
Dr Bank 1,100
Cr Down Payment Received (Sp.G/L A) 1,100
Dr Output Tax Clearing (MVA) 100 ← the offsetting account
Cr Output Tax (MWS) 100

[Step 2] Final invoice
Dr Accounts Receivable 1,100
Cr Revenue 1,000
Cr Output Tax (MWS) 100 ← full amount

[Step 3] Down payment clearing
Dr Down Payment Received 1,100
Cr Accounts Receivable 1,100
Dr Output Tax (MWS) 100
Cr Output Tax Clearing (MVA) 100 ← reverses Step 1
```

Net result: output tax = 100 (final invoice only). The clearing account is a **buffer that prevents double counting**.

| Key | Meaning |
|---|---|
| **MVA** | Output tax clearing on down payments |
| **VVA** | Input tax clearing on down payments |

**For Japan:** down payments received/made are **outside the scope of consumption tax** — tax is triggered by the transfer of assets or provision of services, i.e. at the final invoice. So MVA/VVA are not used. You suppress the tax posting by leaving the **tax category blank on the down payment alternative reconciliation account**, or by assigning a **non-taxable tax code** to the DP request.

SAP explicitly supports the latter pattern — see the Help topic *"How to Set Deviating Tax Codes in Down Payment Requests for Intracommunity Supply"* (ensures no output tax on the DP request while the clearing invoice is taxed with a different code). It's written for EU intracommunity supply, but the "no tax at DP, full tax at final invoice" logic maps directly onto the Japanese requirement.

**Gotcha:** if the DP reconciliation account has tax category `*`, entering a tax code will fail with an account determination error (`FH145` family) unless MVA/VVA are maintained.

**Resulting Japanese flow:**

```
① DP request (F) → no posting (noted item only)
② DP received Dr Bank 1,100 / Cr Down Payment Received 1,100 ← no tax
③ Final invoice Dr AR 1,100 / Cr Revenue 1,000
Cr Output Tax 100 ← full amount here
④ DP clearing Dr Down Payment Received 1,100 / Cr AR 1,100 ← no tax
```

### 3.2 Cash Discount

Per SAP Help, the G/L account configured for cash discount expense/revenue **controls whether a tax adjustment is made**.

```
Dr Accounts Payable 1,100
Cr Bank 1,078
Cr Cash Discount Received 20
Cr Input Tax (VST) 2 ← tax adjustment
```

Rarely used in Japanese business practice, but if used, tax **does** move at clearing. Note also that reversing a clearing where a cash discount triggered a tax adjustment requires a clearing reset — the procedure is more involved than a plain reversal.

### 3.3 Deferred Tax — **not applicable to Japan**

Per SAP Help: in some countries tax is not recognised at invoice issue but only when payment is made; this is called deferred tax, and the system can record and transfer it (dedicated "Deferred Tax Transfer" app).

This is the one genuine "tax moves at clearing" mechanism — used for cash-basis countries (Italy, France, Thailand). **Japan is accrual-basis, so it does not apply.**

### 3.4 Non-Deductible Input Tax — **NAV / NVV**

| Key | Behaviour | Account determination |
|---|---|---|
| **NAV** | Non-deductible input tax posted to a **separate account** | Required |
| **NVV** | Non-deductible input tax **distributed to the expense/asset** | **Not required** |

Per KBA 3263224: NVV is not available in SSCUI 100297 by design — the tax is calculated technically but is not posted to a separate account, so no account determination is needed.

```
[NVV]
Dr Expense 1,100 ← tax absorbed into cost
Cr Accounts Payable 1,100
```

This trips people up — the absence of NVV in 100297 looks like a config gap but isn't.

### 3.5 Foreign Currency / Write-Off

- **FX clearing:** tax is fixed in document currency; only exchange rate difference (KDF) posts. No tax movement.
- **Write-off:** loss posts, but consumption tax adjustment is handled separately (in Japan, bad-debt VAT relief is a declaration-level adjustment).

---

## 4. Tax Declaration Transfer — the actual "tax clearing"

Per SAP Help (*Reporting to the Tax Authority*), there are two methods to credit the tax accounts by transferring amounts to a tax payable account:

**Method 1 — via the Advance Return for Tax program**

The `Advance Return for Tax` job creates a batch input session for automatic transfer posting of tax payable. Per Help: when the session is created, one document is prepared per selected country code, containing one item posting to the tax account and one item posting to the tax payable account, for each tax account.

The tax payable account for this is configured in **SSCUI 100297**:

```
Area : Financial Accounting
Subarea : General Ledger Accounting
Process : Define Tax Accounts
→ Step 2
Transaction Group : STE (Miscellaneous Taxes)
Transaction Key : UMS
Chart of Accounts : YCOA
→ Step 3 → assign the Tax Payable G/L account
```

(This exact path is documented in the Help topic *"Making Additional Settings for VAT Return"* — written for Ukraine, but the SSCUI navigation is generic.)

**Method 2 — manual posting via `Post Tax Payables` (App ID **F1597**)** ← *the approach we're adopting*

Per SAP Help: you use this app to pay tax amounts by posting the balances of input and output tax accounts to a tax payable account. **The app does not require entry of a tax code**, enabling direct manual adjustments to tax accounts.

Key features: save a draft before posting; post tax account balances to a tax payable account; add attachments and notes explaining the reason for the posting.

```
Dr Output Tax 1,000
Cr Input Tax 700
Cr Tax Payable 300 ← amount due
```

### ⚠️ 4.1 F1597 account visibility — the biggest trap I found

Per SAP Help, the settings in `Manage G/L Account Master Data` (SSCUI **150001**) determine whether a G/L account can be found in the `Post Tax Payables` app. **The search result does NOT include the account if any of the following apply:**

- the account is blocked for posting
- the reconciliation type has a value
- **the account is "only automatically posted"**
- the account is not "only automatically posted" **and the tax category has a value other than `>` or `<`**

So to use F1597 on your tax accounts:

| Setting | Required value |
|---|---|
| Blocked for posting | No |
| Reconciliation type | Blank |
| **Only automatically posted** | **Unchecked** |
| **Tax category** | Output tax = `>` / Input tax = `<` |

**This is a direct conflict with common practice.** Tax accounts are usually flagged "only automatically posted" to prevent manual entry — but doing so hides them from F1597. If you want F1597, you must uncheck it, which means the accounts become manually postable from journal entry apps. **Control has to be handled via authorizations (business roles/catalogs) instead.** Worth raising with the customer's internal controls team early.

Also per Help:
- **The tax payable account itself must NOT be tax-relevant** (tax category blank).
- **The tax amounts are not included in the advance report for the following month**, since the generated journal entries contain no tax information. So no double counting — this one is reassuring.

### 4.2 Japan VAT Return

```
Reporting entity : JP_RPG_ENT (Japan Reporting Entity)
Report category : JP_VAT_RETURN (Japan VAT Return)
App : Run Statutory Reports
```

Reporting activities: **`Manage Tax Items`** → **`Prepare VAT Return (Japan)`**.

**Notably, `Tax Payable Posting` is NOT among the reporting activities defined for Japan** — unlike, for example, the Italian or Ukrainian VAT returns, where it is. This appears to confirm that for Japan, SAP expects the tax payable transfer to be done manually via F1597 or a manual journal entry.

**Prerequisite that's easy to miss:** per Help, make sure the **tax reporting date** of financial documents is activated at company code level in the `Enter Global Parameters` configuration activity. For legacy financial documents where the tax reporting date is empty, **you must enter the date manually** — relevant if you're migrating open items via the Migration Cockpit.

---

## 5. SSCUI Map

| # | What | SSCUI / Activity | Location | On-prem equivalent |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **Tax codes** | **101016** — Define Tax Codes for Sales and Purchases | CBC | FTXP |
| 2 | **Tax accounts (MWS/VST/NAV/ESA/ESE)** | **100297** — Automatic Account Determination | Area: Financial Accounting → Subarea: **General Ledger Accounting** → Process: **Define Tax Accounts** → Transaction Group **%TX** | OB40 |
| 3 | **Tax payable account (UMS)** | **100297** | as above, Transaction Group **STE** → Key **UMS** | — |
| 4 | **DP tax clearing (MVA/VVA)** | **100297** *(assumed — see Q1)* | assumed under Subarea: Accounts Receivable / Accounts Payable | **OBXB** |
| 5 | **DP alternative recon. accounts** | SSCUI (Define Recon. Accounts for Customer/Supplier Down Payments) | — | OBXR / OBYR |
| 6 | **G/L master (tax category, auto-post flag)** | **150001** — Manage G/L Account Master Data | — | FS00 |
| 7 | **Tax reporting date activation** | Enter Global Parameters | Company code | OBY6 |
| 8 | **VAT return setup** | Setting Up Your Statutory Reporting / Configure Rules for VAT Return | JP_RPG_ENT / JP_VAT_RETURN | — |

### Transaction key reference

| Group | Key | Description | Account determination |
|---|---|---|---|
| %TX | **MWS** | Output tax | Required |
| %TX | **VST** | Input tax | Required |
| %TX | **NAV** | Non-deductible input tax (separate account) | Required if used |
| %TX | **NVV** | Non-deductible input tax (distributed to cost) | **Not required — by design** |
| %TX | **ESA / ESE** | Acquisition tax (EU) | Not needed for JP |
| **STE** | **UMS** | Tax payable transfer | Only if using automatic transfer |
| — | **MVA** | Output tax clearing on down payments | Not needed for JP |
| — | **VVA** | Input tax clearing on down payments | Not needed for JP |

### 100297 navigation (worked example, per KBA 3016325)

```
1. Open Fiori app "Manage Your Solution"
2. Search for 100297 (Automatic Account Determination) → Configure
3. [Step 1] Area : Financial Accounting
Subarea : General Ledger Accounting
Process : Define Tax Accounts
4. [Step 2] Transaction Group : %TX (Tax on Sales/Purchases)
Transaction Key : VST
Chart of Accounts : YCOA
Tax Code : (your code)
Country : JP
5. [Step 3] Add → enter G/L account
6. Review → Save
```

**Tip:** the Step 3 screen has an "Export to Excel" option — it exports all entries for the selected category even if nothing was changed, which is useful for design document evidence. There's also a released CDS view `I_TRANSACTIONTYPESTDGLACCT` that can be used as a base for a custom view to extract current settings (I haven't verified this myself).

### YCOA accounts

The only YCOA account number I can cite with confidence is **12600000 (Input Tax)**, which appears as the worked example in KBA 3016325. I've deliberately left the others as placeholders rather than guess — see Q4 below.

| Purpose | Transaction key | YCOA account |
|---|---|---|
| Input Tax | VST | **12600000** (per KBA 3016325) |
| Output Tax | MWS | *(to be confirmed)* |
| Tax Payable | UMS | *(to be confirmed)* |
| Non-deductible input tax | NAV | *(to be confirmed)* |

---

## 6. Common Pitfalls (summary)

1. **Create a tax code in 101016 but forget 100297** → `Error in account determination: table T030K key YCOA MWS JP` (see KBA 3339321).
2. **Expecting NVV in 100297** → it's absent by design (KBA 3263224).
3. **Tax accounts flagged "only automatically posted"** → invisible in F1597 (§4.1).
4. **Tax payable account flagged tax-relevant** → rejected by F1597.
5. **Tax reporting date not activated / empty on migrated documents** → items won't appear in the VAT return.
6. **DP reconciliation account with tax category `*` in Japan** → account determination errors for unused MVA/VVA.
7. **Telling the customer the tax settlement is automatic** → it isn't. Set this expectation in Fit-to-Standard, not in UAT.

---

## 7. Open Questions

I'd be grateful for input on the following:

1. **What is the exact SSCUI path for MVA / VVA in Public Edition?**
On-prem it's `OBXB` (SPRO → FI → AR/AP → Business Transactions → Down Payments Made/Received → Define Account for Tax Clearing). I could not find documentation confirming which Subarea/Process exposes these in SSCUI 100297. I've assumed *Subarea: Accounts Receivable / Accounts Payable*. Has anyone maintained these in Public Edition and can confirm?

2. **Can `Tax Payable Posting` be added as a reporting activity to JP_VAT_RETURN?**
Is the activity list fixed by SAP-delivered content, or can it be extended via `Setting Up Your Statutory Reporting`? If it can be added, that would provide an automatic transfer path for Japan.

3. **How are others controlling F1597 access without the "only automatically posted" flag?**
Is business role segregation the only lever, or has anyone found a cleaner approach to protecting tax accounts from manual entry while keeping F1597 usable?

4. **Can anyone confirm the standard YCOA account numbers** for Output Tax (MWS), Tax Payable (UMS), and Non-deductible Input Tax (NAV) in the current Japan Best Practices content? I only have 12600000 for Input Tax from KBA 3016325.

5. **Reduced rate (8%) vs standard rate (10%) — separate G/L accounts?**
100297 allows account determination per tax code, so splitting is technically possible. But since the VAT return aggregates by tax code, I've been advising customers that separate accounts aren't necessary. Has anyone implemented separate accounts, and what drove that decision?

---

## References

**SAP Help Portal — S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition**
Post Tax Payables (F1597) · Advance Return for Tax · Reporting to the Tax Authority · Making Additional Settings for VAT Return · VAT Return (Japan) · Configuration for VAT Return (Japan) · Down Payment Requests · Post Customer Down Payment Requests · Deferred Tax · Cash Discount in Finance · Automatic Postings During Clearing · How to Set Deviating Tax Codes in Down Payment Requests for Intracommunity Supply

**SAP Notes / KBAs**
3009061 (tax code creation, SSCUI 101016) · 3016325 (assigning G/L to tax codes, SSCUI 100297) · 3339321 (T030K account determination error) · 3263224 (NVV in SSCUI 100297) · 2987771 (checking automatic account determination for a G/L account)

---

*Corrections welcome — particularly on the open questions above. Happy to update this post with confirmed answers so it's useful as a reference.*

entry.title: public cloud (update partner function for our customer)
entry.content:
hello all ,

i have task on public cloud to create app to upload excel sheet of customer till bow there is now problem but i need your help on how i can update partner function for our customer so if anyone can help please provide me with it
